bands.label: low
bands.text:
You often find it hard to accurately recognize and name your own emotions as they arise. Reactions can feel like something that just happens, without a clear understanding of why.

Practicing a brief pause before reacting and trying to put a word to the emotion — even approximately — gradually builds this skill.

bands.label: average
bands.text:
You generally understand what you're feeling, although complex or mixed emotions can sometimes be hard to pin down right away.

This is a healthy foundation of self-awareness, with room to deepen it in more complicated emotional situations.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
You're good at recognizing your own emotions as they arise and understanding what triggered them.

This is a valuable foundation for emotional regulation and mindful decision-making, especially under stress.

questions.text: I easily recognize my emotions as they arise.
I understand what triggers my emotional reactions.
It's clear to me why I feel the way I do.
I can put into words what I'm feeling.

bands.label: low
bands.text:
Strong emotions often drive your reaction in the moment, and it can be hard to hold back an impulsive response even when you know you should pause.

Simple techniques — taking a deep breath, a brief pause before responding — gradually strengthen this skill without suppressing the emotions themselves.

bands.label: average
bands.text:
You're able to manage your reactions in most situations, although intense emotions sometimes still get the better of you.

This is a solid baseline skill, with room to grow in especially stressful situations.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
You manage your emotional reactions well even under stress, retaining the ability to act thoughtfully.

This is a valuable skill for roles that require composure and level-headedness under pressure.

questions.text: I can calm myself down even in a stressful situation.
I rarely act impulsively under the influence of emotion.
I can hold back a sharp reaction during a conflict.
I manage to stay calm under pressure.

bands.label: low
bands.text:
You find it hard to intuitively pick up on other people's emotional states, especially when they aren't expressed directly in words.

Deliberate practice — asking directly about feelings, paying attention to nonverbal cues — helps develop this skill over time.

bands.label: average
bands.text:
You generally pick up on the general emotional state of those around you, though finer nuances sometimes go unnoticed.

This is a healthy foundation of empathy, with room to deepen your attention to nonverbal cues.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
You naturally sense other people's emotional states, even when unexpressed, and genuinely empathize with them.

This is a valuable skill for relationships and roles that require understanding other people's needs.

questions.text: I easily pick up on other people's emotional states.
I notice when someone is uncomfortable, even without words.
I can easily put myself in someone else's shoes.
I genuinely empathize with the feelings of those around me.

bands.label: low
bands.text:
Building and maintaining relationships, resolving interpersonal conflicts, or communicating effectively can feel difficult.

This is a skill that develops with practice — small, consistent steps in communication gradually build confidence.

bands.label: average
bands.text:
You generally communicate effectively and build relationships well, though difficult, conflict-laden situations can be challenging.

This is a solid social skill, with room to grow in tense interpersonal situations.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
You build relationships effectively, communicate clearly, and skillfully navigate complex interpersonal situations.

This is a valuable skill for leadership, teamwork, and communication-focused roles.

questions.text: I find it easy to build relationships with new people.
I know how to resolve interpersonal conflicts.
I communicate my thoughts to others clearly.
I find it easy to connect with different kinds of people.

portraits.title: The Emotionally Grounded
portraits.text: You understand your own emotions well and, at the same time, skillfully manage your reactions to them. This combination provides stability and composure even in stressful situations.

portraits.title: The Empathic Communicator
portraits.text: A deep understanding of other people's emotions pairs, in you, with skillful communication. This makes you a natural mediator and someone people find it easy to build trusting relationships with.
